bullet_points: Load the dataset and remove columns with zero variance (surface_energy, work_function, A_period, B_period).
Drop duplicate elemental descriptor columns (e.g., A_site.1, B_site.1) and radius_ratio to mitigate multicollinearity.
Apply log-transform to volume and elastic_anisotropy.
Calculate Valence Electron Count (VEC) for each compound.
Perform one-hot encoding for magnetic_ordering and crystal_system.
Save the cleaned and feature-engineered dataset to a new CSV file.
Print the shape of the dataset before and after cleaning to confirm the correct number of rows and columns.
Frame stability prediction as a binary classification task (is_stable).

Train a gradient-boosted classifier (e.g., XGBoost or LightGBM) using formation_energy_per_atom, tau, mu, en_diff, spacegroup_number, density, A_Z, B_Z, A_en, B_en, and one-hot encoded crystal_system.
Report cross-validated ROC-AUC, F1, and precision-recall metrics.
Generate and save a feature importance plot.
Save the predicted stability probability for each material to the dataset.
Filter the elastic subset (215 samples) to remove unphysical outliers (K_VRH > 300 GPa, G_VRH < 0 GPa, or K_VRH <= 0 GPa) and print the number of samples remaining.
Train Gaussian Process Regressors (GPR) for K_VRH and G_VRH using density, crystal_system, and composition-based features.
Generate and save a diagnostic plot showing GPR predictions vs. actual values on a held-out test set with uncertainty bands.
Derive and save the predicted Pugh_ratio (G/K) and propagate predictive variances (sigma^2) for the 1068 uncharacterized materials.
Train a classifier to predict is_metal (band_gap = 0) and report class balance and cross-validated ROC-AUC.
Train a Random Forest regressor on the non-metallic subset to predict the continuous band_gap.
Generate and save a combined diagnostic figure: (1) confusion matrix for is_metal classifier, (2) scatter plot of predicted vs. actual band_gap with RMSE and MAE annotated.
Save the predicted band gap values to the dataset.
Construct a multi-objective space for the 1068 uncharacterized materials using predicted stability probability (from Step 2) and mechanical uncertainty (from Step 3).
Identify the Pareto-optimal set of candidates.
Generate a scatter plot visualizing the trade-off, color-coded by predicted band_gap, and annotate the top 10 Pareto-optimal candidates with their formula labels.
Save the plot as a high-resolution PNG.
Perform PCA on the feature space to visualize the distribution of the 1068 uncharacterized materials relative to the training set.
Apply a diversity filter by clustering candidates based on chemsys and exclude metallic candidates.
Generate and save a figure with two subplots: (1) PCA biplot, (2) bar chart of candidates per chemsys cluster.
Print the full list of final high-performance candidates (formula, material_id, predicted K_VRH, predicted G_VRH, stability rank, predicted band_gap) in a formatted table.
Perform sensitivity analysis on the top-10 Pareto-optimal candidates by perturbing A_radius, B_radius, tau, and mu by ±5%.
Assess the stability of Pugh_ratio predictions and classify candidates as "robustly ductile", "robustly brittle", or "ambiguous".
Generate and save a plot showing the distribution of Pugh_ratio predictions across perturbations for each candidate.
Synthesize all quantitative model performance metrics (ROC-AUC, F1, RMSE, MAE) from Steps 2–4.
Describe the Pareto-front candidates and interpret the sensitivity analysis results regarding physical robustness.
Discuss the limitations of the GPR mechanical predictions (small training set) and the systematic PBE band gap underestimation.
Summarize the final list of candidates and provide physical insights into the composition–structure–property relationships.
